heroDesc: Forging service for load-bearing and high-strength metal parts
bodyTitle: Metal Forging Service from billet forming to critical face machining
bodyDesc: Metal forging uses pressure forming to improve grain flow and blank density. It is suitable for shafts, flanges, connectors, load-bearing structures and volume metal blanks. Review focuses on material grade, forging route, tooling cost, heat treatment, machining allowance and final inspection standard.

panels.title: Performance Goal
panels.subtitle: Strength / Toughness
panels.body: Suitable for parts requiring material structure and load performance.

panels.title: Manufacturing Route
panels.subtitle: Forging + Finishing
panels.body: Forging creates the blank, then critical faces are machined.

panels.title: Cost Factor
panels.subtitle: Tooling and Volume
panels.body: Higher volume improves the cost case for forged blanks.

tableRows: Selected by material, size and volume
Allowance for holes, faces and threads
Allowance affects material use and cost

steps.title: Material and property review
steps.body: Confirm alloy grade, strength requirement, quantity and critical dimensions.

steps.title: Forging plan
steps.body: Define billet, heating, tooling, allowance and heat treatment route.

steps.title: Forging and heat treatment
steps.body: Complete forming, cooling, cleaning and required heat treatment.

steps.title: Post-machining and inspection
steps.body: Machine critical faces and perform dimensional, hardness or agreed inspection.

asideCta.title: Submit Requirements
asideCta.body: Upload drawings and project goals so engineers can review the service route.

related.title: Related Service Combinations
related.desc: Complex projects often combine multiple services. Review by geometry, volume, material and delivery goals.

items.title: CNC Machining Service
items.body: Milling, turning, mill-turn and hole-pattern machining for metal and engineering plastic parts.

items.title: Molding Service
items.body: Injection molding, tool trials, low-volume molding and design optimization for plastic parts.

items.title: Custom Manufacturing Service
items.body: Coordinates CNC, sheet metal, finishing, purchased parts and assembly for complex projects.

faq.title: Metal Forging Service FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is Metal Forging Service suitable for?
items.a: Metal Forging Service is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a Metal Forging Service quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for Metal Forging Service?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaDesc: Share drawings and requirements — our engineers will recommend a stable process route.
